title: Downgrade Major MongoDB Version for a Cluster

sections.heading: Limitations
sections.body:
The following limitations apply to downgrading your cluster's version:

- You can only downgrade your cluster using the procedure on this page if you
  pinned the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) before upgrading.

- You can only downgrade your cluster to the major version that matches the pinned FCV (Feature Compatibility Version).

- You can only perform this action if the pinned FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) is only one major version below
  the current FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) and you don't have [rapid release](manual/reference/versioning) enabled.

- You can't downgrade to a version after it reaches end-of-life. Ensure pinning occurs
  before the current version reaches end-of-life.

- FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) pinning is only available on Dedicated clusters.

- FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) pinning can't be extended beyond 4 weeks from the pin date.

If a downgrade is necessary, and you did not pin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) before upgrading, you can:

- Restore to a specific point in time:

  - If you have [Continuous Cloud Backup](pit-restore) enabled for your cluster, you can perform a PIT (Point in Time) restore to restore your cluster to a previous state with
    the older MongoDB version:

    - Create a new cluster with the previous major version.

    - Perform a PIT (Point in Time) restore using the instructions in
      [Restore from Continuous Cloud Backup](restore-from-continuous).

- Restore from an Atlas snapshot:

  - If you have [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider) enabled
    without continuous cloud backups, consider restoring the latest snapshot of
    the cluster based on the previous version to a new cluster:

    - Create a new cluster with the previous major version.

    - Restore the latest snapshot before the MongoDB version upgrade to
      the new cluster using the instructions in [Restore from a Scheduled or On-Demand Snapshot](restore-from-snapshot).

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To downgrade a cluster by a major version, pin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for your cluster,
or unpin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for your cluster, you must have the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>)
or higher role for the project.

sections.heading: Pin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for Your Cluster
sections.body:
When your cluster has a pinned FCV (Feature Compatibility Version), a pin icon appears next to your cluster's **Version**
at the bottom of the cluster card. If you hover your cursor over the pin icon, a tooltip that
shows the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) and the expiration date appears.

Atlas unpins your cluster's FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) on the next [Configure Maintenance Window](configure-maintenance-window)
starting on or after the expiration date.

If you already upgraded your cluster's MongoDB version, Atlas upgrades its FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) to match
its MongoDB version after the pin expires.

To pin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for your cluster:

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Click the  icon to the right of the cluster.
Step 2: Select .
sections.body: A modal appears with the **Feature Compatibility Version** and the **Set expiration date** field.

sections.heading: Step 3: Set the expiration date.
sections.body:
All dates are set to Timestamp in ISO 8601 date and time format in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time), in the `MM/DD/YYYY` format. Set the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) expiration date
to be within four weeks of the date to which you pinned the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version). When the expiration date passes, the tooltip displays
`Expired XX/XX/XXXX`. Your FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) pin automatically expires after 4 weeks.

After the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) pin expires, Atlas unpins your cluster's FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) on the next maintenance window starting on or after
the expiration date. If you already upgraded your cluster's MongoDB version, Atlas upgrades its FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) to match its
MongoDB version.

Atlas Administration API

To pin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for your cluster using the Atlas Administration API, see
[Pin FCV for One Cluster from One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-pingroupclusterfeaturecompatibilityversion).

sections.heading: Unpin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for Your Cluster
sections.body:
To unpin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for your cluster:

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Click the  icon to the right of the cluster.
Step 2: Select .
sections.body: A modal appears with the **Feature Compatibility Version** and the **Edit expiration date** field.

sections.heading: Step 3: Click  next to the .
sections.body:
Atlas Administration API

To unpin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for your cluster using the Atlas Administration API, see
[Unpin FCV for One Cluster from One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-unpingroupclusterfeaturecompatibilityversion).

sections.heading: Downgrade Your Cluster by One Major Version
sections.body:
To downgrade your cluster by one major version:

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Click the  icon to the right of the cluster.
Step 3: In the  section of the cluster form, select a major version from the dropdown next to  that matches your cluster's .
sections.body:
A warning banner appears indicating that your cluster's MongoDB version will be downgraded.

Atlas Administration API

To downgrade your cluster by one major version using the API, see
[Modify One Cluster from One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcluster).

sections.heading: Contact Support
sections.body:
If you have any questions regarding migration support beyond what is covered
in this documentation, or if you encounter an error during migration,
file a support ticket through the Atlas user interface.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, reach out to our [support](atlas-support) team.
